name: DOGE Session Drive Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: opening-range or session-window continuation when a specific session structurally dominates follow-through
thesis: DOGE 4h is the best primary lane for testing whether session-driven directional participation persists beyond the initial push when price holds above reclaimed fast value instead of fading immediately back into balance. The edge is continuation after session dominance proves real, not blind breakout chasing.
invalidation_condition: Abort if session-drive holds do not generate enough trades, if follow-through repeatedly dies after the first bar, or if the move is just noise without durable directional persistence.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A dominant session or range window sets directional control, price holds above reclaimed fast value after the drive, and then produces a second continuation leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Transition regimes often show bursts of directional control before the market fully rebalances. DOGE should exaggerate those bursts enough to test a hold-and-go continuation idea.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: DOGE 4h is a suitable primary lane because session-driven pushes are visible at this horizon without depending on lower-timeframe microstructure.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The session impulse may be real but too short-lived, causing entries after the hold to arrive after most of the move is already spent.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If the setup trades but travel is shallow, de-risk faster and shorten holding time before changing the core continuation grammar.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A workable number of continuation entries with expectancy concentrated in the first target and only occasional runner contribution.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: DOGE 4h plus one cross-lane show credible density and positive post-cost continuation after the session-drive hold.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, too-sparse output, or repeated holds that reverse back into balance immediately.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; drive, hold, and persistence are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad post-drive hold logic rather than exact one-bar triggers

validation_basket.why_included: Best lane for visible session-drive behavior in the live basket.
validation_basket.continue_if: Hold-and-go continuation trades with usable density and positive follow-through after costs.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The hold confirms too rarely or follow-through dies immediately.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Repeated directional session pushes followed by a tradable continuation leg.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the mechanism survives on a cleaner major lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH shows the same session-drive hold shape with acceptable density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The pattern only appears on DOGE-style behavior.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but cleaner continuation holds than DOGE.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the mechanism can survive on a more explosive lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO preserves the hold-and-go pattern without collapsing into chaos.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO becomes too violent for reliable continuation entries.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Larger but noisier continuation bursts than DOGE.
